Off Calendar Mar-13-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Mark C. Davis of HASSARD BONNINGTON LLP (415-288-9800), appearing for defendant / moving party John Crane Inc.
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Joseph Anthony Garza

RULING:
DEFENDANT JOHN CRANE INC.'S C.C.P. 1008 MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF DENIAL OF ITS SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION BASED ON NEW FACTS AND CIRCUMSTANCES

Hearing held. The Court took Defendant John Crane Inc.'s motion for reconsideration of denial of its summary judgment motion under submission. Each side submitted a proposed order for the Court's consideration. Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Kimberly L. Avery of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #5074 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY ASBESTOS
Off Calendar May-21-2015 Assigned To Trial Court

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Of Defendant John Crane Inc.
Off Calendar Feb-04-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Mark C. Davis of HASSARD BONNINGTON LLP (415-288-9800), appearing for defendant / moving party John Crane, Inc.
Gilbert L. Purcell of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Joseph Anthony Garza, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT JOHN CRANE, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant John Crane Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. The declarations of Robert Garritano and John Fening create a triable issue whether decedent Joseph Garza was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to decedent's deposition testimony are sustained. Defendant's objections to the Garritano and Fening declarations are overruled. While defendant's Notice of Motion identified a Robert Whalen, defendant's moving papers as a whole, including its memorandum and separate statement, made clear that it was moving for summary judgment on the grounds that there is no evidence that decedent Joseph Garza was exposed to an asbestos-containing John Crane product. (See, e.g., Luri v. Greenwald (2003) 107 Cal.App.4th 1119. 1125.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Holly Sayers of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13678 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Of Defendant John Crane Inc.
Off Calendar Jan-08-2015 Continued To Feb-04-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 503
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Mark C. Davis of HASSARD BONNINGTON LLP (415-288-9800), appearing for defendant / moving party John Crane, Inc.
Oren P. Noah of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Joseph Anthony Garza, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT JOHN CRANE, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant John Crane Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is continued pursuant to plaintiffs' C.C.P. 437c(h) request, which is granted. Plaintiffs disclosed the unidentified former shipmate as Robert Garritano. The Court ordered continued the hearing to February 4, 2015 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 503. Supplemental opposition is due by 1/23 and supplemental reply 1/30. Plaintiff will deliver a copy of the declaration signed by this witness to defendant when it's available. Discovery will remain open as to this witness Garritano only. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Claudia J. Knap of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #6099 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

No argument made and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant John Crane Inc.'s general demurrer to plaintiffs' complaint is sustained with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity, if they are able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
